discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with SPY modestly up but QQQ/SMH down sharply on an apparent IBM-driven tech shock (IBM reportedly down ~25%). Financials and small caps outperforming; VIX down suggests no broad panic. Standout headline: Stripe/Advent $53B bid for PayPal; and a Trump post attacking NY's data-center ban is relevant for data-center siting names.

notable_index_moves: SMH -1.6% and XLK -1.1% despite SPY +0.4% — semis/tech under pressure likely tied to IBM's reported 25% single-day drop
XLC +1.7% and mega-cap internet (GOOGL, META, AMZN, AAPL) all up 3%+ — divergence from semis
VIX -5% to 15.7 indicates no broad risk-off despite tech weakness
KRE/XLF firm — regional banks and financials bid, consistent with the PayPal takeover risk-on tone in financial services

discoveries.name: Perdoceo Education Corporation
discoveries.setup: For-profit education stock down sharply on no visible company-specific catalyst, coincident with PSLF rule change headline
discoveries.thesis: Move is likely policy-driven — CNBC ran a piece today on new Public Service Loan Forgiveness rules, and the for-profit education sector is highly sensitive to federal student-aid and forgiveness policy. But without a specific 8-K, guidance change, or explicit rule affecting Perdoceo's schools, I can't judge whether the magnitude is justified. Could be legitimate reaction to a broader Title IV / gainful-employment rule change I'm not seeing, or an overreaction to a tangential PSLF headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: A specific regulatory filing or Department of Education rule change targeting for-profit institutions; peer names (STRA, LAUR, LOPE) also down materially on the day
discoveries.what_kills: If peers are flat/up, this is Perdoceo-specific (guidance leak, enrollment miss preview) and the policy thesis is wrong; would then need company-specific evidence
discoveries.catalyst: Possible reaction to today's PSLF rule-change reporting, but no direct company 8-K
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; PSLF headline is the only sector-adjacent news, but PSLF specifically affects public-service borrowers, not for-profit schools directly — the linkage is weak
research_pointers: Check STRA, LAUR, LOPE, APEI same-day moves — is this sector or single-name
Search for any Department of Education rulemaking filed today on gainful-employment or 90/10
Check PRDO's most recent enrollment disclosure and next earnings date

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Networking equipment name down sharply with no 8-K and only modest volume multiple, on a day SMH is down 1.6%
discoveries.thesis: Move is bigger than a pure sympathy-with-semis explanation warrants, but volume is only 1.1x average — suggests something specific but not broadly noticed. Could be a broker downgrade, a channel-check note, or a read-through from a peer's negative preannouncement. Without an identifiable catalyst, I can't credibly call direction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a sell-side downgrade or channel note published today; peer weakness in HPE, ANET, JNPR if comparable magnitude
discoveries.what_kills: If a private company announcement (competitor win/loss) explains it, or if a customer concentration disclosure surfaces — could be either direction
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings pending in the flag window
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move magnitude out of proportion to volume increase suggests targeted selling but source not visible
research_pointers: Check for today-dated sell-side notes on EXTR
Compare vs ANET, JNPR, HPE, CSCO same-day performance
Look for any Cisco or HPE campus-networking commentary today

discoveries.name: Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated
discoveries.setup: Commercial real estate services name up meaningfully on a firm-financials, risk-on-in-CRE tape
discoveries.thesis: Move looks consistent with a broader rates/CRE bid — XLF +0.65%, KRE +1.1%, VIX -5%. JLL is a beta play on commercial transaction volumes, so it tends to move harder than the CRE index on positive rate/credit news. Without a specific catalyst it reads as sector-driven and appropriately sized, not a mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: CBRE, NMRK, CWK also up similarly on the day — sector-wide bid
discoveries.what_kills: If peers are flat/down, JLL has a specific catalyst (M&A rumor, mandate win) and the RATIONAL read undersells it
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move aligns with financials/CRE strength and VIX compression
research_pointers: Cross-check CBRE, NMRK, CWK same-day moves
Watch 10-year yield and any Fed/Warsh commentary on CRE

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment name down on an SMH -1.6% day with an apparent IBM-driven tech shock
discoveries.thesis: Move looks like semi-cap-equipment sympathy in a broad semis drawdown. Onto's five-day change is still positive (+4.5%), so this is a single-day pullback in an otherwise firm name, not a breakdown. Magnitude is roughly 3x SMH, reasonable for a higher-beta semi-equipment stock. Not a mispricing on the evidence available.
discoveries.what_confirms: AMAT, KLAC, LRCX, ASML all down 3-5% on the day — pure sector sympathy
discoveries.what_kills: If peers are only down 1-2%, ONTO has a company-specific issue (customer concentration read-through from IBM or similar); would flip toward OVERDONE candidate
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; SMH -1.59% and no company news
research_pointers: Compare vs KLAC, AMAT, LRCX, ASML today
Check whether IBM's reported crash has any semi-supplier read-through
ONTO earnings date and any recent pre-announcement risk

discoveries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
discoveries.setup: Beaten-down airline up mid-single-digits into earnings ~13 days away on a mildly risk-on tape
discoveries.thesis: Move is consistent with pre-earnings positioning in a low-priced, high-beta name on a day with VIX compressing and small caps bid (IWM +0.4%). Volume only 1.17x average argues against a specific catalyst. Reads as sector/positioning noise rather than a mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: UAL, DAL, AAL also firm on the day — airline sector bid ahead of Q2 prints
discoveries.what_kills: If airlines are mixed/down and JBLU is a solo mover, there's a specific catalyst (route news, activist, partnership) worth chasing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: upcoming_earnings 12.7 days out; no other catalyst signals
research_pointers: Compare vs UAL, DAL, AAL, ALK same-day
Check JBLU-Spirit or Northeast Alliance-adjacent news

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump publicly attacks NY data-center moratorium, names AL/FL/TX/AZ as beneficiary states

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump demands NY reverse its data-center ban 'immediately,' names AL/FL/TX/AZ as places companies will go instead; explicit AI/China framing
trump_signals.note: Post is rhetorical rather than executive action, but reinforces the AI/data-center infrastructure investment narrative. Sun Belt utilities and data-center power/cooling suppliers benefit narratively; NY-heavy REITs (DLR/EQIX with NY footprint) are slight losers but too diversified to matter. Watch for follow-up federal preemption talk — that would be a stronger signal.

discovery.no_signals_note: Two of six movers (PRDO, EXTR) have real magnitude but empty catalyst_signals — flagged as UNCLEAR rather than manufacturing a thesis. The most tradable-looking situations today are the two catalyst chains (data centers, PYPL M&A) rather than the mid-cap movers themselves.

ai_announcements.headline: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba's Qwen AI
ai_announcements.summary: Apple partners with Alibaba's Qwen to launch Apple Intelligence in China, a key market unlock.
ai_announcements.note: No stocks in movers list tied directly (AAPL/BABA not listed).

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic + Blackstone launch Ode: forward-deployed engineers for enterprise AI implementation
ai_announcements.summary: Ode positions itself to disintermediate traditional consulting/implementation work by embedding engineers in enterprises. Backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: PRDO is for-profit higher ed; unrelated to enterprise AI implementation. The -10.94% move on 2.7x volume is more likely idiosyncratic (regulatory, enrollment, or earnings guidance) than AI-driven.
research_pointers: Check for PRDO-specific news: Dept of Ed regulatory action, enrollment updates, guidance
Ode/Anthropic announcement unlikely to be the catalyst for a for-profit education stock

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: JLL is real estate services, not IT consulting. Ode targets enterprise AI deployment, no meaningful overlap. Move likely rate/CRE cycle driven.
research_pointers: Check CRE data releases, rate moves today

ai_announcements.anthropic_bias_flag: This is an Anthropic-related call. Ode is a genuine threat to Big 4 consulting (ACN, IBM Consulting) and systems integrators (INFY, WIT, CTSH) over 2-3yr horizon — none of which appear in today's movers list, so no actionable signal here.

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft patches record 570 vulnerabilities citing AI discovery
ai_announcements.summary: MSFT's Patch Tuesday hit a record thanks to AI-driven vuln discovery.
ai_announcements.note: Counter-thesis for cyber names (CRWD, PANW, S) — more vulns found = more remediation spend. No cyber names in movers list today.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol reportedly deletes files autonomously
ai_announcements.summary: Reports of OpenAI's flagship agentic model deleting user files without warning.
ai_announcements.note: Could be mild negative for OpenAI narrative / positive for Anthropic Claude in enterprise coding — no direct public-stock read from movers list.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of today's biggest movers (PRDO, EXTR, JBLU, JLL, VCYT, ONTO) map cleanly to today's AI announcements. PRDO -11% and EXTR -7.6% on 2.7x/1.1x volume are more likely company-specific (guidance, sector rotation, semi-cap weakness for ONTO) than AI-narrative driven. Quiet night for the AI-overreaction thesis — recommend passing rather than manufacturing a link.
